decisions.title: Client-side parsing implementation for Explore V2
decisions.context: Multiple tickets (ENG-9966-9969) suggest moving CSV/Parquet/HDF5/CH10 parsing to client-side for detect_configs. This impacts data import performance and browser resource usage. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' by improving Explore V2 UX.

arguments.for: Reduces server load, faster user feedback, better offline capability, moves computation closer to user
arguments.against: Browser memory constraints, inconsistent parsing across browsers, harder to debug, security surface area

decisions.title: SCIM enterprise features rollout strategy
decisions.context: Multiple SCIM tickets (ENG-9784, 9785, 9926, 9613, 9614) indicate enterprise IdP integration buildout. This is critical for 'Embed Deeper' pillar - enabling larger org deployments. Need decision on GA timeline and pricing tier.

arguments.for: Unblocks enterprise deals, reduces support burden, industry standard expectation, security compliance requirement
arguments.against: Support complexity, ongoing maintenance cost, may delay core product work, limited to subset of customers

decisions.title: GovCloud deployment expansion scope
decisions.context: ENG-9939 shows Windmill deployment in GovCloud for DS workflow management. Signals potential GovCloud customer acquisition. Need to decide if this is strategic bet or one-off. Relates to 'Build the Moat' via compliance differentiation.
decisions.blockedBy: Pending customer commitment confirmation and security certification cost analysis

arguments.for: Opens defense/federal market, high ACVs, strategic moat, less price-sensitive buyers, long contracts
arguments.against: Significant compliance overhead, separate infrastructure costs, limited TAM, slower sales cycles, export controls

decisions.title: Canvas performance architecture - service worker strategy
decisions.context: ENG-9963 splits GeoMaps/ScatterPlot into separate service workers. ENG-9571 shows toolbar rework. ENG-9895 queues status updates. Canvas is key to 'Nail the Core' and decision capture North Star. Need architectural decision on compute distribution.

arguments.for: Better perceived performance, parallel processing, isolates crashes, progressive enhancement possible
arguments.against: Complexity in state management, harder debugging, service worker lifecycle issues, increased memory footprint

decisions.title: Internal tooling consolidation vs fragmentation
decisions.context: ENG-9923 migrates runbooks to internal docs. ENG-8533 adds alerts for internal telemetry. ENG-9613/9614 build IdP onboarding tools. Scattered internal tools may slow team velocity - impacts all pillars indirectly.

arguments.for: Reduces context switching, easier onboarding, single source of truth, better discoverability, standardized workflows
arguments.against: Migration cost, one-size-fits-all limitations, potential tool lock-in, upfront consolidation effort

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load with 32hrs scheduled. Only 8hrs free time across week - below healthy threshold for IC work. Three 1:1s today suggest coordination needs but may fragment focus.
alerts: Meeting density: 80% of work week - recommend blocking focus time
SCIM decision deadline 4/10 has no prep time allocated
